title: veronica bellidioides subsp. lilacina
intro: Veronica bellidioides subsp. lilacina is a subspecies of the Veronica bellidioides plant, a flowering plant native to New Zealand. This plant is also known as the parrot's beak or New Zealand broom, and is valued for its medicinal properties. The leaves and flowers of Veronica bellidioides subsp. lilacina have been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, and are rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds. In herbal preparations, the leaves and flowers can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness their medicinal properties and provide health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing.
